items.project_definition: The primary interface facilitating communication between the client and the server for course management operations.
A validation rule preventing the deletion of a learning resource if users are currently registered to it.
The total set of defined test cases that must pass to satisfy deployment criteria.
A non-blocking HTTP utility used within the test suite to simulate requests to the application.
A non-blocking database connection context used for transactional data operations.
An asynchronous execution mechanism that triggers email dispatch after the HTTP response is sent.
A specific exception type mapped to a 409 Conflict response when a domain constraint is breached.
The security protocol managing cross-origin resource sharing for the web interface.
The four foundational persistent storage mutation primitives.
An exception raised when mandatory environment variables, such as the transactional mail key, are absent.
A Pydantic data transfer object defining the required fields for initiating a new educational resource.
A Pydantic data transfer object outlining the public and internal data exposed when retrieving educational resources.
A Pydantic data transfer object specifying the modifiable fields for an existing educational resource.
The process of transforming passwords into non-reversible strings using the bcrypt algorithm.
The PostgreSQL persistent storage instance managing the relational schema.
The FastAPI injection system providing database sessions and authenticated user contexts to route handlers.
A Pydantic data transfer object used to link a student to a specific educational resource.
A Pydantic data transfer object returning the state and progress of a student's registration in a resource.
A Pydantic data transfer object used to modify the progress percentage and completion date.
A relational constraint ensuring referential integrity between tables, such as linking modules to a parent resource.
A high-level functional block of the system, specifically the course management suite.
The standard mechanism for returning specific error codes like 401 or 403 to the client.
A unique identifier for every record, implemented as a universally unique alphanumeric string.
The security boundary ensuring students can only access their own registration and progress data.
The signed token format used for stateless authentication and role verification.
The minimum set of functional requirements including database, auth, and basic course management for initial delivery.
A software layer that intercepts all outbound responses to wrap them in a standardized envelope.
A Pydantic data transfer object for defining a segment of an educational resource.
A Pydantic data transfer object containing the metadata and ordering of an educational resource segment.
A logical negation used to defer email dispatch during the registration flow.
The abstraction layer mapping Python classes to PostgreSQL tables.
The capability to develop domain routers for instructors and students concurrently after the foundation is laid.
An exception triggered when an authenticated user lacks the required role or ownership to perform an action.
The operational act of triggering a software exception to halt execution and signal an error.
The external documentation links providing the full specification and planning details.
The process of sending a finalized payload and HTTP status code back to the calling client.
The provided Python library for interacting with the external transactional mail service.
The specific version of the database toolkit used for asynchronous interaction with PostgreSQL.
The minimum acceptable threshold of 80% for business logic test coverage.
The set of verifiable outcomes required to mark a phase as complete.
A Pydantic data transfer object containing short-term access and long-term refresh strings.
The data type used for primary keys to ensure unique identification across distributed environments.
A Pydantic data transfer object capturing credentials for identity verification.
A Pydantic data transfer object containing the necessary information to create a new account.
A Pydantic data transfer object returning sanitized profile information.
An exception raised by the data validation library when a payload does not match the expected schema.
An exception raised when a numeric value, such as progress, falls outside the 0-100 range.
The command used to bootstrap the database migration environment with async support.
